summary: All 3 v1 findings resolved. Fresh-eyes review of Parse() changes found 1 minor contract gap: ToText() doesn't produce `\` escapes for multiline step text, so the claimed 'Inverse of ToText()' contract is broken. Not a runtime bug — nobody round-trips ToText→Parse in production. The `//` comment simplification and `\` escape feature are both clean and well-tested. The `ExcludesProviderProperties` test is a good addition. No critical or major issues.

previous_reviews_assessed.codeanalyzer: agree — v5 correctly identified all 4 changes as clean, backslash escape analysis is thorough
previous_reviews_assessed.tester: n/a — no tester review of this cycle
previous_reviews_assessed.security: n/a — no security review of this cycle

findings.issue: Parse() docstring claims 'Inverse of ToText()' (line 196) but ToText() (line 142) doesn't produce `\` escapes for multiline step text. A step with Text='first line\ncontinuation' renders via ToText as raw newlines. Re-parsing that output treats the unindented second line as a goal header, not a continuation. Additionally, ToText doesn't emit space-indented continuation lines either.
findings.impact: Low. ToText() is only called in 4 test methods — never in production. The builder pipeline uses FormatForLlm (template-based), not ToText. But the false contract could mislead future developers who trust the docstring.
findings.suggestion: Either fix ToText to emit continuation lines (space-indented or `\`-escaped), or update the docstring to remove the 'Inverse' claim. Recommend the docstring fix since ToText isn't used in production.
